title: Recognizing the significant impact and legacy of Cecil Corbin-Mark in the environmental justice community and further recognizing that climate change most severely impacts vulnerable and disadvantaged communities in the United States and around the world, and that it is the responsibility of the United States Government to work with its global partners to promote environmental justice.
plain_summary: This bill honors Cecil Corbin-Mark, a person who worked hard to help communities with environmental problems. The bill says that climate change hurts poor people and minority communities more than others. It asks the United States government to work with other countries to make sure everyone is treated fairly when it comes to environmental issues. The bill also wants the government to take quick action to reduce pollution that causes climate change.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Preamble
sections.text: This resolution honors Cecil Corbin-Mark who worked to help communities that face environmental problems. The resolution also says that climate change hurts poor and minority communities the most. It states that the United States should work with other countries to make sure all people are treated fairly when dealing with environmental issues.

sections.title: Findings
sections.text: Climate change is a very serious threat that could end human life as we know it. The world is already seeing dangerous effects from climate change happening right now. Changes in the Earth's weather patterns create real dangers for the United States and countries everywhere. We need countries around the world to work together to deal with climate change and prepare for emergencies. Climate change makes other problems worse and can lead to fights between countries. It causes droughts when there is no rain, floods, hurricanes, heat waves, fires, natural disasters, and not enough food. These problems make people fight over water and food, force people to leave their homes, and create refugee crises. The Paris Agreement is a deal between countries to fight climate change. It says that when countries take action on climate change, they must respect human rights, the right to health, the rights of native peoples, local communities, migrants, children, people with disabilities, and people in dangerous situations. It also mentions the right to development, treating men and women equally, empowering women, and being fair to future generations. The Paris Agreement talks about climate justice, which means being fair when dealing with climate change. It says we need a strong response to the urgent threat of climate change. A special report from climate scientists says that communities that already face hardships include communities of color, native peoples, and farming communities. These groups are hurt more by climate change than others. The World Health Organization found that seven million people die too early each year from diseases made worse by air pollution. Air pollution is a major cause of climate change. About ninety percent of these deaths happen in poor and middle income countries. The World Health Organization says air pollution is the biggest environmental threat to health. They estimate that between two thousand thirty and two thousand fifty, climate change will cause two hundred fifty thousand extra deaths each year from not having enough food, malaria, diarrhea, and heat stress. The gap between rich and poor people and their health will get bigger as climate change gets worse. Heat islands are city areas with little green space that stay very hot. These areas harm the health of people of color and elderly people more than others. Access to clean water in the United States and around the world is badly affected by climate change. This hurts communities of color who already have trouble getting clean water. Studies show that terrible hurricanes hitting the United States and Caribbean countries in recent years have been made worse by climate change. Families in the dry corridor area of Honduras, Guatemala, and El Salvador lost up to eighty percent of their corn and bean crops in both growing seasons of two thousand eighteen because of drought. Rising temperatures, more extreme weather, and unpredictable rainfall patterns mess up farming cycles. This puts Central American farmers out of work and forces them to move to other places. In southern Africa, crop losses made worse by climate change would increase food prices by an average of twelve percent by two thousand thirty. This would be very hard on poor families who spend as much as sixty percent of their money on food. The resulting hunger could lead to a twenty three percent increase in severe stunting, which means children do not grow and develop normally. Eight hundred million people in South Asia depend on water from the Himalaya mountains. As temperatures get warmer and ice melts away, droughts and reduced water flow threaten Nepal's tourism industry and the lives of rural farmers. Small Island Developing States are countries made up of small islands that face serious health risks from climate change. Climate change increases flooding because sea levels rise and exposes people to infectious diseases because freshwater supplies get contaminated. Cecil Corbin-Mark was the Deputy Director of WE ACT for Environmental Justice. He spent his career helping environmental justice communities from his home in Harlem to around the world. He fought against unfair treatment faced by communities of color.

sections.title: Resolution
sections.text: The House of Representatives recognizes the important work and lasting impact of Cecil Corbin-Mark in the environmental justice community. It also recognizes that climate change hurts vulnerable and disadvantaged communities in the United States and around the world the most. The United States Government has a responsibility to work with other countries to promote environmental justice and climate justice. The House recognizes that all efforts to adapt to and reduce climate change must include specific protections for communities of color, native peoples, and other frontline communities around the world. It also acknowledges the harm done to these communities. The House recognizes that reducing climate change must be a global effort in which the United States should be a leader among other countries. The House urges the United States Government to increase collaboration and cooperation with other countries. They should pursue policies that focus on helping vulnerable and disadvantaged communities adapt to climate change. This is important because climate change affects minority communities more, even though they are least responsible for causing climate change but suffer the most from its effects. The House says all countries need to promote adaptation that focuses on vulnerable communities in all areas including farming, infrastructure, and health. The House recognizes and encourages all countries to include all stakeholders when developing policies to address environmental justice and climate justice. The House says immediate action by multiple countries is needed to drastically reduce global greenhouse gas emissions in order to reduce the effects of climate change.
